2019/015.rationale: Assigned Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards because the project studies household saving behaviour using the Wealth and Assets Survey; Descriptive Monitoring because 'understanding saving behaviour' indicates profiling patterns and characteristics of saving rather than testing a named intervention; no tag because no pandemic framing or central demographic comparison is evident.

2019/024.rationale: Assigned Housing & Planning and Migration & Demographics because the project examines the Right to Buy housing policy in relation to migration and gentrification using the ONS Longitudinal Study; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the Right to Buy is a specific named policy whose consequences are being analysed; no tag because no pandemic framing or central demographic equality comparison is present.

2020/003.rationale: Assigned Housing & Planning, Labour Market & Employment and Public Finance & Taxation because the project studies spatial sorting in housing and employment driven by public investments, planning decisions, housing subsidies and business rates; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the title explicitly assesses the impact of named policy instruments; no tag because the COVID-19 business survey appears as a data source rather than a central pandemic lens.

2020/006.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because the outcome is educational inequality and disadvantage across cohorts; Outcome Tracking because parental separation is a naturally occurring family exposure linked to downstream educational outcomes using the ONS Longitudinal Study; no tag because family structure comparison is not a demographic equality-group comparison and no pandemic framing exists.

2020/013.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project analyses firm-level research and development using business R&D expenditure, business structure and patent data; Descriptive Monitoring because the title indicates characterising firm R&D activity rather than testing a specific exposure or named intervention; no tag because the COVID-19 linked survey is incidental to the visible research question.

2020/019.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment and Public Finance & Taxation because the project studies how government expenditure affects employment and wages; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the analytical operation estimates the impact of deliberate public spending on firm-level labour outcomes; no tag because no pandemic or demographic equality lens is central.

2020/022.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills and Labour Market & Employment because the project analyses digital skills within English apprenticeship standards and their relation to medium and associate professional job roles, using ASHE earnings data; Descriptive Monitoring because the visible operation is profiling skills content across job roles rather than testing an intervention; no tag because no pandemic or demographic comparison is central.

2020/027.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the COVID-19 Infection Survey measures infection, immunisation and mortality outcomes; Descriptive Monitoring because the survey's operation is population surveillance of infection prevalence and linked deaths over time; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because COVID-19 infection is the explicit and central object of the study.

2020/030/b.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because occupational mobility is the substantive focus; Life-Course / Trajectory Analysis because the ONS Longitudinal Study is used to follow individuals' occupational movement over extended time; no tag because no pandemic framing or central demographic equality comparison is evident.

2020/030/a.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project examines firm engagement in design and exporting using the UK Innovation Survey; Outcome Tracking because it links a naturally occurring firm characteristic (engaging in design) to a downstream firm outcome (exporting); no tag because no pandemic or demographic lens is present.

2020/033.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because the title centres on education in relation to identity using the Labour Force Survey; Outcome Tracking because the design links educational participation/attainment to identity outcomes rather than evaluating a named policy; Demographic disparities tag because identity (national/ethnic group affiliation) is central to the comparative research question.

2020/045.rationale: Assigned Migration & Demographics and Public Finance & Taxation because the title studies migration and its relationship to redistributive local-authority spending; Outcome Tracking because migration is a naturally occurring exposure linked to a public-spending outcome rather than a named policy; no tag because no demographic-comparison or pandemic lens is central.

2020/046.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the title concerns wage and employment dynamics using ASHE, New Earnings Survey and linked earnings data; Life-Course / Trajectory Analysis because 'dynamics' with longitudinal linked earnings data indicates following workers' wage and employment histories over time; no tag because no equity or pandemic lens is evident.

2020/050.rationale: Assigned Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards because the title frames the project around inequalities in living conditions and resources using the ONS Longitudinal Study; Descriptive Monitoring because the visible operation is characterising the level and pattern of inequalities over the 21st century; Demographic disparities tag because comparison of outcomes across population groups is the central framing of an 'inequalities' study on census-linked data.

2020/053.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project studies firms' decisions to become multinational using business surveys, FDI and R&D data; Outcome Tracking because it links domestic economic conditions and incentives (naturally occurring exposures) to the firm-level outcome of multinational status; no tag because no pandemic or demographic-comparison lens applies.

2020/056.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because adherence to stay-at-home public health guidance is the substantive public-health object; Outcome Tracking because the study analyses factors associated with adherence rather than building a prospective prediction tool or evaluating a named intervention; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because the pandemic and its guidance are the explicit condition of the research question.

2020/062.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity and Labour Market & Employment because the project examines productivity and inclusivity outcomes using the Business Structure Database alongside APS, ASHE and BRES employment/earnings data; Outcome Tracking because it links local institutions (a naturally occurring exposure) to productivity, sustainability and inclusivity outcomes; no tag because no pandemic or demographic-equity lens is central.

2020/066.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the object is the Competition and Markets Authority's regulatory action on markets and businesses, using the Business Impact of COVID-19 Survey; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the title explicitly evaluates a named taskforce; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because the taskforce and datasets are explicitly pandemic-focused.

2020/074.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the substantive focus is the health of older people across places using the ONS Longitudinal Study; Descriptive Monitoring because the visible operation is characterising how older people's health varies across places rather than testing a specific exposure or evaluating a named policy; no tag because age defines the study population rather than a central group comparison and no pandemic framing appears.

2020/077.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because place of death in older adults is an end-of-life health/care outcome studied with the ONS Longitudinal Study; Outcome Tracking because the design links naturally occurring social determinants to the downstream outcome of place of death; no tag because socioeconomic determinants alone do not constitute a demographic-equity comparison and no pandemic lens is present.

2020/078.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the study examines health and mortality outcomes as its research object, using the ONS Longitudinal Study; Outcome Tracking because it links a naturally occurring occupational status (postal work) to downstream health and mortality outcomes; no tag because no demographic-equity comparison or pandemic lens is central.

2020/082.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because wellbeing is the substantive outcome studied via the Coronavirus social impacts survey; Descriptive Monitoring and Outcome Tracking because the project charts wellbeing changes over time and across groups while linking pandemic conditions to wellbeing; COVID-19 tag because pandemic effects are the explicit condition, and demographic disparities tag because comparison by demographic characteristics is central to the title.

2020/083.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because labour market outcomes are the explicit object of study across 1991-2011; Outcome Tracking because religion, a naturally occurring characteristic, is linked to labour-market outcomes; demographic disparities tag because comparison across religious groups is central to the research question.

2020/084.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because excess mortality is analysed as a health outcome using death registration data; Descriptive Monitoring because the project measures levels and trends of excess deaths during and after the pandemic; COVID-19 tag because the pandemic is the explicit framing condition.

2020/086.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment and Business & Productivity because the study examines labour supply shocks and firm outcomes using earnings, business survey, and vacancy data; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the exposure is the UK's immigration policy, a deliberate policy change; no tag because no demographic-equity comparison or pandemic lens is central.

2020/092.rationale: Assigned Migration & Demographics because fertility patterns in the UK are the substantive object, and Data Infrastructure & Methodology because improving fertility forecasting methodologies is itself an object of the project; Descriptive Monitoring because recent fertility is characterised, and Methodological / Infrastructure Research because forecasting methods are being developed and improved; no tag because no equity comparison or pandemic condition is central.

2020/095.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because firm productivity and trade are the substantive outcomes, using business surveys, R&D and trade-in-goods data; Outcome Tracking because policy uncertainty is a naturally occurring economic condition, not a named policy intervention, linked to firm outcomes; no tag because no demographic-equity comparison or pandemic lens is central.

2020/104.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because COVID-19 infection in schools, linked to immunisation and Test & Trace data, is the substantive object while schools are only the setting; Descriptive Monitoring because the project analyses infection survey results to characterise infection levels for government decision making; COVID-19 tag because pandemic infection surveillance is explicitly central.

2020/106.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because non-coronavirus deaths are analysed as a health/mortality outcome using death registration extracts; Descriptive Monitoring because the project profiles death registrations over a defined period; COVID-19 tag because the pandemic context explicitly frames the analysis of deaths not involving coronavirus.

2020/118.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because multimorbidity, polypharmacy, health, resilience and wellbeing outcomes in the Welsh population are the substantive focus; Outcome Tracking because phenotypes are linked to poorer downstream outcomes, and Methodological / Infrastructure Research because discovering new phenotype classifications via machine learning is itself a central analytical contribution; no tag because no equity comparison or pandemic framing is central.

2020/120.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care, Labour Market & Employment and Business & Productivity because the local area profiles combine infection/death data with APS/ASHE earnings and the Business Structure Database; Descriptive Monitoring because 'profiles' indicates characterising levels and patterns across local areas rather than testing exposures; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because the pandemic is the explicit condition being profiled.

2020/127.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care and Labour Market & Employment because the project studies the health of domiciliary care workers as a workforce for service planning and carer support; Outcome Tracking because it links COVID-19 exposure to downstream health outcomes for these workers; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because COVID-19 impact is the central research condition.

2021/002.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the project examines social and wellbeing impacts of coronavirus using the Coronavirus and Social Impacts survey; Descriptive Monitoring because the design is profiling social impacts on the London population without an exposure-outcome test or named intervention; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because coronavirus impacts are the explicit focus.

2021/012.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the project investigates epidemiological patterns of COVID-19 infection using the Infection Survey and linked death data; Descriptive Monitoring because 'epidemiological insights across the UK' indicates measuring infection prevalence and patterns rather than evaluating an intervention; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because infection surveillance is the core subject.

2021/013.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because COVID-19 mortality is the health outcome studied via death registrations and linked census-death data; Outcome Tracking because ethnicity, socioeconomic position and geography are analysed as exposures linked to mortality outcomes; COVID-19 & Pandemic and demographic disparities tags because pandemic mortality and ethnic inequality are both explicit and central to the title.

2021/015.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment, Business & Productivity and Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards because the economic impact on residents and businesses is studied via LFS, BICS and Effects of Tax and Benefits data; Descriptive Monitoring because the project profiles how pandemic impacts are unequally distributed across a local area rather than testing a named intervention; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because pandemic economic impact is central; no demographic tag because the inequalities are place- and socioeconomic-based rather than centrally across equality-relevant groups.

2021/017.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the ISCF Medicines Manufacturing programme concerns firm-level innovation and performance, evidenced by ABS, BERD and Business Structure Database usage; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the title explicitly states 'Evaluation' of a named funding programme; no tag because neither pandemic nor demographic comparison is central.

2021/018.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because working lives are the substantive outcome studied via the Labour Force Survey and BICS; Outcome Tracking because the project links the naturally occurring pandemic exposure to labour-market outcomes; COVID-19 & Pandemic and demographic disparities tags because the pandemic is the exposure and the gender comparison between women and men is central to the research question.

2021/028.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the project concerns healthcare resource allocation and unmet need, using death registration data; Methodological / Infrastructure Research because 'accounting for unmet need' indicates developing or improving allocation measurement frameworks; no tag because equity here concerns need-based allocation rather than a central demographic-group comparison or pandemic lens.

2021/032.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the project studies non-monetary work benefits and the gender pay gap using ASHE earnings data; Descriptive Monitoring because the analysis characterises how including non-wage benefits changes the measured pay gap rather than testing an intervention; demographic disparities tag because the gender comparison of pay is central to the research question.

2021/040.rationale: Assigned Crime & Justice because the project studies serious and organised crime cases before the Crown Courts using MoJ court defendant and prisoner custodial journey datasets; Descriptive Monitoring and Service Interaction / Systems Analysis because it measures the nature and extent of cases and follows defendant case progression through magistrates', Crown court and custodial systems; no tag because no demographic-comparison or pandemic lens is evident.

2021/044.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care and Labour Market & Employment because the project links occupation to COVID-19 infection using the Covid-19 Infection Survey; Outcome Tracking because occupational exposure is analysed against infection outcomes rather than a named intervention; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because COVID-19 infection is the central research object.

2021/046.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the project concerns palliative and end of life care using death registrations and health research databases; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis and Methodological / Infrastructure Research because it evaluates the pandemic response in palliative care while explicitly aiming to boost data assets; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because the pandemic response is the explicit evaluation object.

2021/053.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment and Education & Skills because the project studies occupational changes and skill mismatch among graduates using Labour Force Survey and APS data; Outcome Tracking because it links the naturally occurring COVID-19 shock to occupational and skills-matching outcomes; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because pandemic-driven change is the central exposure.

2021/054.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the distributional impact assessment draws on Annual Population Survey employment and population data around the rail investment; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because Northern Powerhouse Rail is a named public investment scheme whose impacts are being assessed; Demographic disparities / equity tag because a distributional impact assessment centrally compares impacts across population groups.

2021/071.rationale: Assigned Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards because the project focuses on poverty and inequality within Central London; Descriptive Monitoring and Outcome Tracking because it profiles poverty and inequality patterns and links them to the impact of the pandemic; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because pandemic impact is explicit and central in the title.

2021/087.rationale: Assigned Migration & Demographics and Labour Market & Employment because spatial mobility (population movement) and social mobility (occupational progression) are the substantive objects, using the ONS Longitudinal Study; Life-Course / Trajectory Analysis because the longitudinal study design follows individuals' mobility trajectories over extended periods; no tag because geographical inequality, not demographic-group comparison or pandemic, frames the study.

2021/089.rationale: Assigned Crime & Justice because scam victimisation is the substantive focus, using the Crime Survey for England and Wales; Outcome Tracking because the project links the naturally occurring exposure of being scammed to subjective wellbeing outcomes; no tag because neither demographic comparison nor pandemic conditions are central.

2021/090.rationale: Assigned Environment & Agriculture and Data Infrastructure & Methodology because the project builds linked individual and farm-level data for agricultural research using SAIL and census/APS Wales data; Methodological / Infrastructure Research because the linkage of data assets is itself the central operation of this workstream; no tag because no demographic-equity or pandemic lens is evident.

2021/104.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the project studies impacts on the self-employed using Labour Force Survey and APS data; Outcome Tracking because it links the COVID-19 shock to spatial and social outcomes for the self-employed; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because pandemic impacts are the central exposure, and Demographic disparities / equity tag because the title centres inclusivity in how impacts vary across social groups.

2021/114.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care and Environment & Agriculture because the project studies health and well-being impacts of urban air quality interventions; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because Low Emission and Clean Air Zones are named policy interventions whose impacts are being evaluated; no tag because no demographic-comparison or pandemic lens is evident.

2021/123.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the 'People research strand' of the Economy 2030 Inquiry draws heavily on labour force, earnings, and vacancy surveys to study workers and employment change; Descriptive Monitoring because an inquiry strand of this kind characterises levels, trends, and composition of the labour market rather than testing a single exposure or named policy; no tag because the coronavirus dataset appears as one context source rather than a central pandemic lens.

2021/131.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because precarious employment among men is the substantive outcome, analysed with Labour Force Survey data; Descriptive Monitoring because the analysis profiles the prevalence and patterns of precarious work across groups; Demographic disparities / equity tag because comparison by ethnicity is central to the research question.

2021/132.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project studies foreign market power in the UK economy using firm-level business structure and trade-in-goods data; Descriptive Monitoring because the visible operation is measuring the extent and importance of foreign market power rather than evaluating a named policy; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is present.

2021/137.rationale: Assigned Data Infrastructure & Methodology because the UK Social Value Bank is itself a measurement and valuation asset being built from survey data (Crime Survey, Understanding Society); Methodological / Infrastructure Research because the analytical operation is developing and maintaining a bank of social-value measures; no tag because no demographic-comparison or pandemic lens is central.

2021/138.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity and Data Infrastructure & Methodology because the project characterises the industrial structure of the Shetland Islands economy by constructing input-output tables, a statistical infrastructure product, from business survey data; Methodological / Infrastructure Research because the central analytical operation is building the input-output tables themselves; no tag because no equity or pandemic lens is evident.

2021/140.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because wages, hours of work, and income are central objects, and Health & Social Care because well-being is the outcome of interest; Outcome Tracking because the design links naturally occurring labour-market circumstances (wages, hours, income) to well-being outcomes; no tag because no demographic-comparison or pandemic framing is present.

2021/154.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because occupational sorting by task content and English language proficiency is a labour-market outcome, studied with Census data; Descriptive Monitoring because the operation characterises patterns of occupational sorting across groups; Demographic disparities / equity tag because comparison between immigrants, UK-born ethnic minorities, and White British is central to the research question.

2021/172.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the project studies the UK labour market using earnings (ASHE) and Labour Force Survey data; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the framing 'impact of institutional change' indicates estimating the effects of deliberate institutional reforms on labour-market outcomes; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is central.

2021/176.rationale: Assigned Environment & Agriculture because the project concerns sustainable and circular energy transitions in the Scottish Islands using meter-level gas and electricity consumption data; Descriptive Monitoring because the visible operation is measuring and characterising local energy consumption patterns to inform the transition; no tag because no equity or pandemic lens is evident.

2021/180.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project studies firm innovation and export performance using the Longitudinal Small Business Survey; Outcome Tracking because it links a naturally occurring firm characteristic (innovation) to a downstream outcome (exports); no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is evident.

2022/022.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care and Crime & Justice because the project studies births and birth outcomes (maternity/health outcomes) within the women's prison estate (a justice institution) using birth registrations; Descriptive Monitoring because it measures and characterises births and outcomes in this population rather than testing an intervention; no tag because neither demographic-group comparison nor pandemic framing is central.

2022/041.rationale: Assigned Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards because digital poverty concerns material deprivation and access to resources across UK households; Descriptive Monitoring because the design combines multiple household and labour surveys to measure the prevalence and distribution of digital poverty; no tag because although one dataset is pandemic-related, COVID-19 is not central to the research question and no demographic comparison is signalled.

2022/044.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity and Labour Market & Employment because the project examines entrepreneurial firms and employment effects of the 'China Shock' using the Small Business Survey and Labour Force Survey; Outcome Tracking because it links a naturally occurring trade exposure to firm and labour-market outcomes rather than evaluating a named policy; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is central.

2022/045.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project studies creative-industry clusters, spillovers and growth using business, R&D and innovation surveys; Outcome Tracking because it links clustering (a naturally occurring condition) to spillover and growth outcomes rather than evaluating a named intervention; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is evident.

2022/047.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills and Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards because the project studies children's academic and behavioural outcomes under the Universal Credit benefit reform using Understanding Society; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because Universal Credit is a named policy whose effects are being estimated; no tag because no demographic-group comparison or pandemic framing is central.

2022/060.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project evaluates ERDF business support using the Business Structure Database and earnings data with firms as the object of analysis; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the title explicitly frames an evaluation of a named support programme; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is evident.

2022/073.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because the project studies the disadvantage attainment gap using the Growing Up in England education-linked cohort data; Outcome Tracking because it links naturally occurring family circumstances (parental education and occupation) to attainment outcomes; no tag because the comparison is socioeconomic rather than across a demographic or equality-relevant group.

2022/095.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project analyses the productivity slowdown at the firm level using the Annual Business Survey and Annual Respondents Database; Descriptive Monitoring because the 'micro-anatomy' framing indicates decomposing and characterising the slowdown's patterns rather than testing a policy or single exposure; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is central.

2022/109.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project studies startups and their regional distribution using the Business Structure Database and Annual Business Survey; Descriptive Monitoring because it characterises how startup activity varies across regions in relation to regional inequality rather than evaluating a named intervention; no tag because regional inequality is not a demographic or equality-relevant group comparison and no pandemic framing appears.

2022/117.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because the project studies access and representation in elite higher education using UCAS admissions data; Descriptive Monitoring because the visible operation is profiling who accesses and is represented in elite institutions; Demographic disparities tag because access and representation framing centres comparison across equality-relevant groups.

2022/129.rationale: Assigned Data Infrastructure & Methodology and Environment & Agriculture because AD|ARC involves creating linked individual-farm data assets for agricultural research using the Agricultural Research Collection; Methodological / Infrastructure Research because the central operation is building and demonstrating the linked data infrastructure; no tag because no demographic-comparison or pandemic lens is evident.

2022/132.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project concerns Northern Ireland trade and exports using the Annual Business Inquiry and Broad Economy Sales and Exports Statistics; Methodological / Infrastructure Research because the visible operation is constructing a trade model as an analytical tool; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is present.

2022/133.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the project measures earnings differentials using the Earnings and Employees Study; Descriptive Monitoring because it characterises the level and pattern of earnings gaps in 2011; Demographic disparities tag because the Catholic-Protestant religious comparison is the central research question.

2022/135.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because wage inequality and automation's effect on workers are central, using the Annual Survey of Hours and Earnings; Outcome Tracking because the design links automation and regulatory exposure to downstream wage outcomes rather than evaluating a named policy; no tag because no demographic-group comparison or pandemic framing is central.

2022/136.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because firm entry, exit, and aggregate productivity are the substantive objects, using the Annual Business Survey and Annual Respondents Database; Outcome Tracking because the design links naturally occurring firm entry and exit dynamics to productivity outcomes; no tag because no equity or pandemic lens appears.

2022/144.rationale: Assigned Housing & Planning and Labour Market & Employment because the project studies how the housing market shapes the spatial allocation of credit-constrained workers, using ASHE and Understanding Society; Outcome Tracking because it links housing-market conditions to worker location and labour outcomes; no tag because no demographic comparison or pandemic condition is central.

2022/159.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment and Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards because the project studies relationships between paid employment, deprivation and unpaid care work using the ONS Longitudinal Study; Outcome Tracking because it links unpaid care and deprivation circumstances to employment outcomes rather than only profiling them; no tag because no demographic-group comparison or pandemic lens is stated as central.

2022/172.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because innovation, management capability and SME productivity are the substantive objects, using multiple firm-level surveys including the UK Innovation Survey and Management Practices Survey; Outcome Tracking because the design links innovation and management practices to firm productivity outcomes; no tag because no equity or pandemic lens is present.

2022/176.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity and Labour Market & Employment because the project examines how skill deficiencies affect firm performance, productivity and agglomeration, using employer skills surveys, business surveys and the Labour Force Survey; Outcome Tracking because it links local skill deficiencies as an exposure to firm and local economic outcomes; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is central.

2022/177.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the title concerns firm innovation and technology management and the datasets are firm-level business, R&D, productivity and innovation surveys; Descriptive Monitoring because the entry indicates characterising innovation and technology-management patterns across firms rather than a named intervention or exposure-outcome test; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is evident.

2022/193.rationale: Assigned Crime & Justice because the project studies reoffending using MoJ courts, prisons and probation data; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because it evaluates whether the enforced alcohol abstinence intervention reduces reoffending; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is central.

2023/004.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills and Health & Social Care because the study links looked-after (children's social care) status to educational pathways and outcomes; Life-Course / Trajectory Analysis and Outcome Tracking because it follows children's educational pathways and links care status to downstream educational outcomes; no tag because care status is not a demographic or pandemic lens.

2023/008.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because elite mobility concerns occupational structure and mobility studied via the Labour Force Survey; Life-Course / Trajectory Analysis because social/elite mobility involves following individuals' progression into elite positions over time; no tag because no equality-group comparison or pandemic framing is central.

2023/009.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project studies trade and firm performance using firm-level business, FDI and trade datasets; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because Brexit is a deliberate institutional change whose effects on firms are being assessed; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is evident.

2023/031.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because monopsony and minimum wages concern wages and labour-market power, using ASHE and APS earnings data; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the minimum wage is a named policy whose effects under monopsony are analysed; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is central.

2023/033.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because the project studies the UK higher education market and admissions using UCAS data; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the focus on competition and regulation indicates assessing the effects of regulatory arrangements on the HE market; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is central.

2023/040.rationale: Assigned Crime & Justice because the outcome is domestic violence victimisation measured in the Crime Survey for England and Wales; Outcome Tracking because female council representation is a naturally occurring exposure linked to domestic violence outcomes rather than a named intervention; no tag because gender here defines the exposure rather than a comparison of outcomes across demographic groups.

2023/046.rationale: Assigned Migration & Demographics because immigrants' location choices and population movement are the substantive object; Outcome Tracking because local far-right support is a naturally occurring exposure linked to immigrants' settlement decisions; no tag because immigrant status defines the study population rather than a demographic-comparison lens.

2023/056.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because children's social services in Wales are the substantive system studied; Descriptive Monitoring and Service Interaction / Systems Analysis because the project characterises patterns and outcomes of contact with and processing by children's social services; Demographic disparities / equity tag because ethnic and religious inequalities are the central comparative lens of the research question.

2023/066.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the project studies COVID-19 vaccination uptake using infection survey and vaccination datasets; Descriptive Monitoring because the analytical operation is characterising heterogeneity in vaccination across social networks rather than testing an exposure-outcome relationship; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because vaccination and pandemic infection surveillance data are central to the research question.

2023/073.rationale: Assigned Crime & Justice because domestic abuse proceedings and private/public family justice using MoJ FamilyMan court data are the substantive focus; Service Interaction / Systems Analysis because the study examines overlap and pathways of cases through the family court system; no tag because no demographic-comparison or pandemic lens is indicated.

2023/094.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because health outcomes of Scottish nurses are the substantive object; Outcome Tracking because the design links deprivation, a naturally occurring circumstance, to downstream health outcomes; no tag because deprivation is a socioeconomic rather than demographic or equality-relevant comparison and no pandemic framing appears.

2023/098.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills and Health & Social Care because the project links time spent in care (Children Looked After, Children in Need data) to educational attainment at ages 16-18; Outcome Tracking because time in care is a naturally occurring circumstance linked to a downstream attainment outcome rather than a named intervention; no tag because no central demographic comparison or pandemic condition is evident.

2023/113.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because later-life health and morbidity are the outcomes of interest; Outcome Tracking and Life-Course / Trajectory Analysis because early-life health and nutritional environment are linked to later-life outcomes across the life span using the ONS Longitudinal Study; no tag because neither demographic comparison nor pandemic framing is central.

2023/114.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment and Education & Skills because occupational sorting and earnings stability are studied as consequences of compulsory education, using ASHE and LFS earnings data; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because compulsory education is a deliberate policy whose impact is being estimated; no tag because no demographic-equity or pandemic lens is present.

2023/167.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity and Environment & Agriculture because the project analyses firm market power along the agricultural milk supply chain using the Annual Respondents Database; Descriptive Monitoring because the visible operation is measuring and characterising market power rather than evaluating a policy or testing an exposure-outcome link; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is indicated.

2023/170.rationale: Assigned Public Finance & Taxation and Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards because the tax-benefit system and household resources (Family Resources Survey, Households Below Average Income, expenditure surveys) are the substantive objects; Methodological / Infrastructure Research and Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because microsimulation involves building and maintaining a modelling tool used to estimate distributional impacts of tax and benefit policies; no tag because no demographic-equity or pandemic framing is central.

2023/173.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because university grade inflation and attainment are the substantive focus using Longitudinal Education Outcomes data; Descriptive Monitoring and Outcome Tracking because the project both characterises trends in grade inflation and links university attainment to subsequent outcomes; no tag because no demographic comparison or pandemic condition is central.

2023/181.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity and Labour Market & Employment because Enterprise Zone impacts on firms (Business Structure Database, Annual Business Survey) and on employment and earnings (ASHE, LFS) are studied; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the project explicitly assesses the impact of a named policy, UK Enterprise Zones; no tag because no demographic-equity or pandemic lens appears.

2023/190.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity and Labour Market & Employment because the title centres on understanding productivity at the macroeconomic level using earnings and labour-force datasets (ASHE, LFS); Descriptive Monitoring because 'understanding productivity' indicates measuring and characterising productivity patterns rather than testing a specific exposure or policy; no tag because no demographic-comparison or pandemic lens is evident.

2023/204.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project studies firm-level R&D, innovation and productivity using business surveys (ABS, BERD, UK Innovation Survey); Descriptive Monitoring because 'understanding' firm-level patterns in a regional economy indicates profiling and characterisation rather than policy evaluation or exposure-outcome testing; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens appears.

2023/205.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because educational attainment is the central outcome, supported by Growing Up in England and school census data; Outcome Tracking because the design links family background (a naturally occurring circumstance) to attainment outcomes; Demographic disparities tag because comparison across ethnic categories is explicit in the title.
